title: Tres Leches Cake
description: A light sponge cake soaked in three milks until impossibly moist, topped with whipped cream. A Mexican dessert classic.

steps.instruction: Preheat oven to 350°F. Grease and flour a 9x13 inch baking pan.
Beat egg yolks with ¾ cup sugar until thick and pale. Mix in milk and vanilla.
Fold in flour and baking powder gently.
In a separate bowl, beat egg whites to soft peaks. Gradually add ¼ cup sugar and beat to stiff peaks.
Fold egg whites into batter in three additions. Pour into prepared pan.
Bake 25–30 minutes until a toothpick comes out clean. Cool completely in pan.
Using a fork or skewer, poke holes all over the top of the cooled cake — approximately every ½ inch.
Whisk together evaporated milk, condensed milk, and heavy cream. Pour slowly over the cake, allowing it to absorb fully. Refrigerate at least 2 hours (overnight preferred).
Whip heavy cream with powdered sugar and vanilla to soft peaks. Spread over the fully soaked cake.
Slice and serve chilled. Trim any dry edges before serving for the best texture.

notes: Overnight refrigeration is strongly preferred — the longer it soaks, the more moist and flavorful the cake becomes. Always trim the dry browned edges before serving.
